title: My First Mineral Hunt
overview: High school girl Ruri meets graduate student Nagi while collecting minerals in the mountains.

title: The Value of Golden Color
overview: Ruri is determined to find gold, but Nagi explains that it might not be as likely as she hopes.

title: The Lost Planet
overview: Ruri meets Nagi's colleague Imari, and the three embark on a journey to find a lost mine.

title: Study of Sands
overview: In search of sapphire, Nagi teaches Ruri how to study sand, but Ruri prefers to hunt on a bigger scale.

title: Worlds Seen and Unseen
overview: Ruri, Nagi, and Imari head to the beach in search of agate, and Ruri learns about the long lifecycle of rocks.

title: A Closer Look into the Blue
overview: Just as the group begins searching for sapphire on foot, Ruri uncovers a grave mistake that halts their plans.

title: Seaside Recycling Studio
overview: Ruri finds sea glass on the beach and runs into her classmate Shoko, who has a secret passion for minerals.

title: The Golden Elegy
overview: Ruri teams up with Shoko for a science project, which takes a turn when they discover a strange, orange rock.

title: Time Capsule of 1.9 Million Tons
overview: Ruri and Nagi head out to hunt for opals but discover that the area has already been fully excavated.

title: Sentence to an Abandoned Railroad
overview: Imari leads a hunt for manganese ore, when the group encounters a mystery along abandoned railroad tracks.

title: The Sapphire Cradle
overview: When Ruri has trouble deciding on her future, she turns to her study of sapphire.

title: Memories Come with Rocks and Noise
overview: When Ruri discovers her grandpa's old crystal radio, she's determined to restore it.

title: Look Up, Examine, Search! And Next!
overview: Ruri, Shoko, Nagi, and Imari take a trip together, where they search for meteorite at a hot springs inn.
